# Preface {.unnumbered}

This reader accompanies the lecture **Supply Chain Management (SCM)** at Hochschule RheinMain, taught in the 3rd semester of the Bachelor program.

## Course Philosophy

Supply chain management sits at the intersection of strategy, operations, and quantitative modeling. This course takes a deliberate dual approach:

-   **Quantitative rigor**: We formulate mathematical models, derive analytical solutions, and implement algorithms in R. Understanding the math is not optional — it is the foundation for sound management decisions.
-   **Management relevance**: Every model has a story. We always ask: *What does this solution mean for the manager?* *What are the limits of the model?* *What happens when assumptions break down?*

## How to Use This Reader

Each chapter corresponds to one lecture module. You will find:

-   **Conceptual explanations** with examples from practice
-   **Mathematical formulations** in clean notation
-   **R code** implementing models and visualizations — all code is reproducible
-   **Exercises** at the end of each chapter for practice
-   **Further reading** pointers to Chopra & Meindl chapters

## Prerequisites

-   Basic linear algebra and probability theory
-   Introductory R programming (or willingness to learn)
-   First-semester microeconomics (optimization concepts)

## Main Textbook

Chopra, S., & Meindl, P. (latest edition). *Supply Chain Management: Strategy, Planning, and Operation*. Pearson.

Supplementary:

-   Silver, E., Pyke, D., & Thomas, D. — *Inventory and Production Management in Supply Chains* (CRC Press)
-   Simchi-Levi, D. — *Operations Rules* (MIT Press)
-   Thonemann, U. — *Operations Management* (Pearson Studium)

## Software

All R code in this reader uses:

``` r
library(tidyverse)        # data wrangling and visualization
library(lpSolve)          # linear and transportation problems
library(ompr)             # MILP modeling (DIY algebraic modeling)
library(ompr.roi)         # solver interface
library(ROI.plugin.glpk)  # GLPK solver
library(plotly)           # interactive visualizations
library(kableExtra)       # formatted tables
```

Install with:

``` r
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "lpSolve", "ompr", "ompr.roi",
                   "ROI.plugin.glpk", "plotly", "kableExtra"))
```
